parameters.query:
SELECT u.id AS user_id, e.id AS enrolment_id, e.current_module_id,
       m.title AS module_title, m.content AS module_content,
       c.title AS course_title
FROM users u
JOIN enrolments e ON e.user_id = u.id
JOIN modules m ON m.id = e.current_module_id
JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id
WHERE u.slack_user_id = $1
ORDER BY e.enrolled_at DESC
LIMIT 1;

body.text: You don't have an active module. Use `/enroll <course-code>` to join a course first.

options.systemMessage: You are a fair, encouraging quiz evaluator for a corporate LMS. Be constructive and specific.

parameters.query:
WITH progress_insert AS (
  INSERT INTO progress (user_id, module_id)
  VALUES ($1, $2)
  ON CONFLICT (user_id, module_id) DO NOTHING
),
next_module AS (
  SELECT id AS next_id FROM modules
  WHERE course_id = (SELECT course_id FROM modules WHERE id = $2)
    AND position > (SELECT position FROM modules WHERE id = $2)
  ORDER BY position ASC
  LIMIT 1
)
UPDATE enrolments
SET
  current_module_id = (SELECT next_id FROM next_module),
  completed_at = CASE
    WHEN (SELECT next_id FROM next_module) IS NULL THEN NOW()
    ELSE completed_at
  END
WHERE id = $3
RETURNING current_module_id, completed_at;

text.text: Use `/cert` to claim your certificate of completion!
Your progress has been saved. Use `/learn` to continue to your next lesson.

parameters.query:
SELECT u.id AS user_id, e.id AS enrolment_id,
       COALESCE(e.current_module_id, m_first.id) AS current_module_id,
       m.title AS module_title, m.content AS module_content,
       m.position AS module_position,
       c.title AS course_title, c.id AS course_id,
       (
         SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id
       ) AS total_modules,
       (
         SELECT COUNT(*) FROM progress p2
         JOIN modules m2 ON m2.id = p2.module_id
         WHERE p2.user_id = u.id AND m2.course_id = c.id
       ) AS completed_modules
FROM users u
JOIN enrolments e ON e.user_id = u.id
JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id
LEFT JOIN modules m ON m.id = e.current_module_id
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT id FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id ORDER BY position ASC LIMIT 1
) m_first ON true
WHERE u.slack_user_id = $1
  AND e.completed_at IS NULL
ORDER BY e.enrolled_at DESC
LIMIT 1;

parameters.query:
SELECT
  u.id AS user_id,
  u.display_name,
  COALESCE(
    (
      SELECT json_agg(json_build_object(
        'course', c.title,
        'course_code', c.code,
        'enrolled_at', e.enrolled_at,
        'completed_at', e.completed_at,
        'total_modules', (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id),
        'completed_modules', (
          SELECT COUNT(*) FROM progress p
          JOIN modules m ON m.id = p.module_id
          WHERE p.user_id = u.id AND m.course_id = c.id
        ),
        'current_module', (SELECT title FROM modules WHERE id = e.current_module_id)
      ))
      FROM enrolments e
      JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id
      WHERE e.user_id = u.id
    ),
    '[]'::json
  ) AS enrolments,
  (
    SELECT COUNT(*) FROM certificates WHERE user_id = u.id
  ) AS certificates_earned
FROM users u
WHERE u.slack_user_id = $1;

parameters.query:
SELECT
  c.code,
  c.title,
  c.description,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id) AS module_count,
  CASE WHEN c.code LIKE 'OB-%' THEN true ELSE false END AS is_onboarding
FROM courses c
ORDER BY is_onboarding DESC, c.code;

elements.text: {{ $json.course_count }} course(s) available · Use `/enroll <CODE>` to join a course

text.text:
*Learning Commands*
`/learn` — Start or resume your current lesson
`/learn <N>` — Jump to lesson number N
`/submit <answer>` — Submit your answer for the current module
`/progress` — View your learning progress across all courses
`/courses` — Browse all available courses

*Enrolment Commands*
`/enroll <CODE>` — Enrol in a course using its code
`/unenroll <CODE>` — Leave a course
`/onboard` — Run the onboarding workflow (auto-enrols in OB courses)

*Achievement Commands*
`/cert` — Claim your certificate once all modules are complete
`/report` — View a summary report of your team's learning
`/gaps` — Identify skill gaps in your learning path

*Admin Commands*
`/backup` — Trigger a Google Sheets backup of all LMS data _(admin only)_

elements.text: Need more help? Contact your LMS admin or visit the #lms-support channel.

parameters.query:
SELECT
  u.id AS user_id,
  u.display_name,
  u.email,
  e.id AS enrolment_id,
  c.id AS course_id,
  c.title AS course_title,
  c.code AS course_code,
  (
    SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id
  ) AS total_modules,
  (
    SELECT COUNT(*) FROM progress p
    JOIN modules m ON m.id = p.module_id
    WHERE p.user_id = u.id AND m.course_id = c.id
  ) AS completed_modules,
  (
    SELECT id FROM certificates
    WHERE user_id = u.id AND course_id = c.id
  ) AS existing_cert_id
FROM users u
JOIN enrolments e ON e.user_id = u.id
JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id
WHERE u.slack_user_id = $1
ORDER BY e.enrolled_at DESC
LIMIT 1;

You've completed {{ $json.completed_modules }}/{{ $json.total_modules }} modules in *{{ $json.course_title }}*. Finish all modules first, then use `/cert` to claim your certificate.

parameters.query:
WITH cert AS (
  INSERT INTO certificates (user_id, course_id)
  VALUES ($1, $2)
  ON CONFLICT (user_id, course_id) DO NOTHING
  RETURNING id, issued_at
),
enrol_update AS (
  UPDATE enrolments SET completed_at = NOW()
  WHERE id = $3 AND completed_at IS NULL
  RETURNING id
)
SELECT id, TO_CHAR(issued_at, 'DD Month YYYY') AS issued_at_formatted FROM cert;

Please provide a course code. Usage: `/enroll <COURSE-CODE>`

Use `/courses` to browse available courses, or ask your LMS admin for a code.

parameters.query:
WITH upsert AS (
  INSERT INTO users (slack_user_id, slack_team_id, display_name)
  VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
  ON CONFLICT (slack_user_id) DO UPDATE SET display_name = EXCLUDED.display_name
  RETURNING id
)
SELECT
  c.id AS course_id,
  c.title AS course_title,
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.description AS course_description,
  m.id AS first_module_id,
  m.title AS first_module_title,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id) AS total_modules,
  (SELECT id FROM upsert) AS user_id,
  (
    SELECT id FROM enrolments e2
    WHERE e2.user_id = (SELECT id FROM upsert)
      AND e2.course_id = c.id
  ) AS existing_enrolment_id
FROM courses c
LEFT JOIN modules m ON m.id = (
  SELECT id FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id ORDER BY position ASC LIMIT 1
)
WHERE UPPER(c.code) = $4;

parameters.query:
-- Stuck learners: enrolled but no progress in the last 7 days
SELECT
  u.display_name,
  u.slack_user_id,
  c.title AS course_title,
  m.title AS stuck_module,
  m.position + 1 AS module_number,
  (
    SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id
  ) AS total_modules,
  (
    SELECT MAX(completed_at) FROM progress p2
    JOIN modules m2 ON m2.id = p2.module_id
    WHERE p2.user_id = u.id AND m2.course_id = c.id
  ) AS last_progress_at,
  e.enrolled_at
FROM enrolments e
JOIN users u ON u.id = e.user_id
JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id
JOIN modules m ON m.id = e.current_module_id
WHERE e.completed_at IS NULL
  AND (
    (
      SELECT MAX(completed_at) FROM progress p3
      JOIN modules m3 ON m3.id = p3.module_id
      WHERE p3.user_id = u.id AND m3.course_id = c.id
    ) < NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
    OR (
      SELECT COUNT(*) FROM progress p4
      JOIN modules m4 ON m4.id = p4.module_id
      WHERE p4.user_id = u.id AND m4.course_id = c.id
    ) = 0
  )
ORDER BY e.enrolled_at ASC
LIMIT 20;

-- Hard modules: modules with lowest pass rate
SELECT
  m.title AS module_title,
  m.position + 1 AS module_number,
  c.title AS course_title,
  COUNT(DISTINCT e.user_id) AS learners_reached,
  COUNT(DISTINCT p.user_id) AS learners_passed,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT p.user_id) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT e.user_id), 0),
    1
  ) AS pass_rate_pct
FROM modules m
JOIN courses c ON c.id = m.course_id
LEFT JOIN enrolments e ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN progress p ON p.module_id = m.id AND p.user_id = e.user_id
GROUP BY m.id, m.title, m.position, c.title
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT e.user_id) >= 2
ORDER BY pass_rate_pct ASC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 5;

parameters.text:
=You are the Gap Analyst for the RWR Group LMS. You identify learning gaps and recommend interventions.

STUCK LEARNERS (no progress in 7+ days):
{{ JSON.stringify($('Get Stuck Learners').all().map(i => i.json)) }}

HARD MODULES (lowest pass rates):
{{ JSON.stringify($('Get Hard Modules').all().map(i => i.json)) }}

Requested by admin: {{ $('When Called by Supervisor').item.json.user_name }}

Provide a concise gap analysis report in Slack mrkdwn format:

*Section 1: Stuck Learners Summary*
- Total count and which courses/modules they're stuck on
- Pattern analysis (e.g. 'Most stuck on Module 3 of Sales Training')
- Specific names/modules in a brief list (max 5, then 'and N more')
- Recommended intervention (e.g. peer pairing, manager check-in)

*Section 2: Hard Modules*
- Modules with lowest pass rates
- Hypothesis about why they're hard
- Content improvement recommendation

*Section 3: Quick Wins*
- 2-3 actionable recommendations an admin can do today

Keep total under 1500 characters.

options.systemMessage: You are a data-driven L&D analyst. Be specific, actionable, and concise.

body.text:
You have been unenrolled from *{{ $('Lookup Enrolment').item.json.course_title }}*. Your progress records have been removed.

Use `/courses` to find other courses, or `/enroll {{ $('Lookup Enrolment').item.json.course_code }}` to re-enrol.

parameters.query:
SELECT
  u.slack_user_id,
  u.display_name,
  c.title AS course_title,
  m.title AS current_module_title,
  e.current_module_id AS lesson_id,
  (
    SELECT COUNT(*) FROM progress p
    JOIN modules m2 ON m2.id = p.module_id
    WHERE p.user_id = u.id AND m2.course_id = c.id
  ) AS completed_modules,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id) AS total_modules,
  (
    SELECT MAX(p.completed_at) FROM progress p
    JOIN modules m2 ON m2.id = p.module_id
    WHERE p.user_id = u.id AND m2.course_id = c.id
  ) AS last_activity
FROM users u
JOIN enrolments e ON e.user_id = u.id AND e.completed_at IS NULL
JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id
LEFT JOIN modules m ON m.id = e.current_module_id
WHERE
  -- No module completed in the last 3 days
  NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM progress p
    JOIN modules m2 ON m2.id = p.module_id
    WHERE p.user_id = u.id
      AND m2.course_id = c.id
      AND p.completed_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '3 days'
  )
  -- Re-send daily only when there is no recent reaction to prior nudges
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM nudge_reactions nr
    WHERE nr.user_id = u.id
      AND nr.reacted_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
  )
ORDER BY u.display_name;

elements.text: Use the buttons to react. Tap 📝 Assignment to open a submission form (link or screenshot) that is auto-named and saved to Drive.

parameters.query:
SELECT
  -- Enrolment stats
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enrolments) AS total_enrolments,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enrolments WHERE completed_at IS NOT NULL) AS completed_enrolments,
  (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM enrolments) AS unique_learners,
  (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM enrolments WHERE completed_at IS NOT NULL) AS learners_completed_a_course,

  -- Active learners (progress in last 7 days)
  (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM progress WHERE completed_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days') AS active_learners_7d,

  -- Completion rate
  ROUND(
    100.0 * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enrolments WHERE completed_at IS NOT NULL)
    / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enrolments), 0), 1
  ) AS completion_rate_pct,

  -- Certificates
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM certificates) AS total_certificates,

  -- Top courses by enrolment
  (
    SELECT json_agg(json_build_object(
      'course', c.title,
      'code', c.code,
      'enrolments', cnt,
      'completions', compl
    ) ORDER BY cnt DESC)
    FROM (
      SELECT
        e.course_id,
        COUNT(*) AS cnt,
        COUNT(e.completed_at) AS compl
      FROM enrolments e
      GROUP BY e.course_id
      LIMIT 5
    ) t
    JOIN courses c ON c.id = t.course_id
  ) AS top_courses,

  -- Modules completed today
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM progress WHERE completed_at::date = CURRENT_DATE) AS modules_completed_today,

  -- New enrolments this week
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enrolments WHERE enrolled_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days') AS new_enrolments_7d;

options.systemMessage: You are a sharp L&D analytics expert. Be data-driven, precise, and actionable.

elements.text: Generated {{ new Date().toLocaleString() }} · Requested by {{ $('When Called by Supervisor').item.json.user_name }}

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO users (slack_user_id, slack_team_id, display_name)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
ON CONFLICT (slack_user_id) DO UPDATE
  SET display_name = EXCLUDED.display_name,
      updated_at = NOW()
RETURNING id, display_name, created_at,
  (created_at = updated_at) AS is_new_user;

SELECT
  c.id AS course_id,
  c.title,
  c.code,
  c.description,
  (
    SELECT id FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id ORDER BY position ASC LIMIT 1
  ) AS first_module_id,
  (
    SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id
  ) AS total_modules,
  (
    SELECT id FROM enrolments
    WHERE user_id = $1
      AND course_id = c.id
  ) AS existing_enrolment_id
FROM courses c
WHERE c.code LIKE 'OB-%'
ORDER BY c.code;

INSERT INTO enrolments (user_id, course_id, current_module_id)
SELECT
  $1,
  c.id,
  (SELECT id FROM modules WHERE course_id = c.id ORDER BY position ASC LIMIT 1)
FROM courses c
WHERE c.code LIKE 'OB-%'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM enrolments e2
    WHERE e2.user_id = $1
      AND e2.course_id = c.id
  )
RETURNING course_id;

parameters.text:
=You are the Onboarding Agent for RWR Group's LMS, welcoming a new employee.

New employee: {{ $('When Called by Supervisor').item.json.user_name }}
Is brand new user: {{ $('Upsert User').item.json.is_new_user }}
Onboarding courses enrolled in: {{ JSON.stringify($('Get Onboarding Courses').all().map(i => ({ title: i.json.title, code: i.json.code, modules: i.json.total_modules }))) }}
Enrolled in N new courses: {{ $('Auto-Enrol in OB Courses').all().length }}

Write a warm, professional Slack onboarding welcome message in mrkdwn format.
Include:
1. Personal welcome greeting using their name
2. Brief intro to the LMS purpose at RWR Group (1-2 sentences)
3. Their learning path — list each onboarding course with a brief one-line description of why it matters
4. Step-by-step quick-start guide:
   • Step 1: Use /learn to start your first lesson
   • Step 2: Complete the mission with /submit
   • Step 3: Track progress with /progress
   • Step 4: Earn your certificate with /cert
5. Encouraging closing line

Tone: warm, welcoming, professional. Under 1000 characters total.

options.systemMessage: You are a warm, professional corporate onboarding specialist. Make the new employee feel welcomed and confident.

fields.text:
*Courses enrolled:*
{{ $('Get Onboarding Courses').all().map(i => '• ' + i.json.title + ' (' + i.json.total_modules + ' modules)').join('\n') }}

*Quick commands:*
`/learn` — Start a lesson
`/submit` — Complete a mission
`/progress` — View your stats
`/cert` — Claim a certificate

parameters.query:
SELECT
  u.slack_user_id,
  u.display_name,
  c.title  AS course_title,
  m.title  AS module_title,
  TO_CHAR(p.completed_at, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS completed_at
FROM progress p
JOIN users   u ON u.id = p.user_id
JOIN modules m ON m.id = p.module_id
JOIN courses c ON c.id = m.course_id
ORDER BY p.completed_at DESC

SELECT
  u.slack_user_id,
  u.display_name,
  c.code   AS course_code,
  c.title  AS course_title,
  TO_CHAR(e.enrolled_at,  'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS enrolled_at,
  CASE WHEN e.completed_at IS NOT NULL
       THEN TO_CHAR(e.completed_at, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')
       ELSE NULL END                               AS completed_at,
  CASE WHEN e.completed_at IS NOT NULL
       THEN 'Completed' ELSE 'In Progress' END     AS status
FROM enrolments e
JOIN users   u ON u.id = e.user_id
JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id
ORDER BY e.enrolled_at DESC

SELECT
  u.slack_user_id,
  u.display_name,
  c.code   AS course_code,
  c.title  AS course_title,
  TO_CHAR(cert.issued_at, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS issued_at
FROM certificates cert
JOIN users   u ON u.id = cert.user_id
JOIN courses c ON c.id = cert.course_id
ORDER BY cert.issued_at DESC

SELECT
  table_name,
  row_count,
  TO_CHAR(NOW(), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS snapshot_at
FROM (
  SELECT 'users'        AS table_name, COUNT(*)::int AS row_count FROM users
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'courses',                    COUNT(*)      FROM courses
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'modules',                    COUNT(*)      FROM modules
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'enrolments',                 COUNT(*)      FROM enrolments
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'progress',                   COUNT(*)      FROM progress
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'certificates',               COUNT(*)      FROM certificates
) counts
SELECT id, slack_user_id FROM users WHERE slack_user_id = $1 LIMIT 1;
INSERT INTO nudge_reactions (user_id, lesson_id, reaction_type, payload) VALUES ($1, NULLIF($2,'')::INT, $3, $4::jsonb);
